message.content:
T7 — FL-05: Inspector family type picker

  You are working in the monorepo at /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai.
  Your working directory for most edits is packages/web.

  ## Task: FL-05 — Inspector family type picker for door + window

  ### Branch
  git checkout main && git pull && git checkout -b fl-05-inspector-type-picker

  ### Context

  FL-02 is merged. The catalog is at:
    packages/web/src/families/familyCatalog.ts
    exports: BUILT_IN_FAMILIES: FamilyDefinition[], getFamilyById(), getTypeById()

  FamilyDefinition.defaultTypes entries each have: { id, name, familyId, discipline, parameters, isBuiltIn }

  The Inspector currently shows door/window fields in InspectorPropertiesFor() in:
    packages/web/src/workspace/InspectorContent.tsx  (line 59 for door, line 68 for window)

  Both are currently read-only FieldRow displays. The door case shows:
    <FieldRow label={f('family')} value={el.familyTypeId ?? 'Generic 900 × 2100'} mono />
    <FieldRow label={f('width')} value={fmtMm(el.widthMm)} />
    <FieldRow label={f('wall')} value={el.wallId} mono />
    <FieldRow label={f('alongT')} value={el.alongT.toFixed(3)} mono />

  The window case shows:
    <FieldRow label={f('family')} value={el.familyTypeId ?? 'Generic 1200 × 1500'} mono />
    + width, height, sillHeight, wall

  How other editable editors work — follow this exact pattern:
    InspectorRoomEditor in InspectorContent.tsx receives { el, revision, onPersistProperty }
    onPersistProperty(key: string, value: string) → caller dispatches updateElementProperty command

  In RedesignedWorkspace.tsx (~line 1228) it is wired as:
    el.kind === 'room' ?
      <InspectorRoomEditor el={el} revision={revision} onPersistProperty={(key, value) =>
        void onSemanticCommand({ type: 'updateElementProperty', elementId: el.id, key, value })
      } />

  The Properties tab routing chain in RedesignedWorkspace.tsx (~line 1214-1253):
    if (el.kind === 'plan_view') → InspectorPlanViewEditor
    else if (el.kind === 'room') → InspectorRoomEditor
    else if (el.kind === 'viewpoint') → InspectorViewpointEditor
    else if (el.kind === 'view_template') → InspectorViewTemplateEditor
    else → InspectorPropertiesFor(el, t)   ← door/window currently fall through here

  CSS classes already in use (copy these for consistency):
    INPUT_CLS = 'mt-1 w-full rounded border border-border bg-background px-2 py-1 font-mono text-[11px]'
    LABEL_CLS = 'block text-[10px] text-muted'

  ### What to build

  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  FILE 1: packages/web/src/workspace/InspectorContent.tsx — add two new exports
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  Add these imports at the top:
    import { BUILT_IN_FAMILIES } from '../families/familyCatalog';

  Add new exported component InspectorDoorEditor:

    export function InspectorDoorEditor({
      el, revision, onPersistProperty,
    }: {
      el: Extract<Element, { kind: 'door' }>;
      revision: number;
      onPersistProperty: (key: string, value: string) => void;
    }): JSX.Element

    Renders:
      - A <select> labelled "Family type" populated with:
          <option value="">Generic</option>
          {BUILT_IN_FAMILIES that have discipline === 'door', for each family show an <optgroup label={family.name}>,
           and inside each optgroup render one <option value={type.id}>{type.name}</option> per defaultType}
        The select's current value = el.familyTypeId ?? ''
        onChange: onPersistProperty('familyTypeId', e.target.value)
      - Read-only FieldRow for width (fmtMm(el.widthMm))
      - Read-only FieldRow for wall (el.wallId, mono)
      - Read-only FieldRow for position (el.alongT.toFixed(3), mono)

  Add new exported component InspectorWindowEditor with the same pattern:
    - <select> for familyTypeId, discipline === 'window' families
    - onChange: onPersistProperty('familyTypeId', e.target.value)
    - Read-only FieldRows: width, height, sillHeight, wall

  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  FILE 2: packages/web/src/workspace/RedesignedWorkspace.tsx — wire the new editors
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  Add InspectorDoorEditor and InspectorWindowEditor to the import from InspectorContent.

  In the Properties tab routing chain (around line 1228), insert before the final
  `InspectorPropertiesFor(el, t)` fallthrough:

    ... existing plan_view / room / viewpoint / view_template branches ...
    : el.kind === 'door' ? (
      <InspectorDoorEditor
        el={el}
        revision={revision}
        onPersistProperty={(key, value) =>
          void onSemanticCommand({ type: 'updateElementProperty', elementId: el.id, key, value })
        }
      />
    ) : el.kind === 'window' ? (
      <InspectorWindowEditor
        el={el}
        revision={revision}
        onPersistProperty={(key, value) =>
          void onSemanticCommand({ type: 'updateElementProperty', elementId: el.id, key, value })
        }
      />
    ) : (
      InspectorPropertiesFor(el, t)
    )

  ### Acceptance criteria
  1. pnpm -r typecheck passes.
  2. pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web test passes (1177 tests).
  3. Only InspectorContent.tsx and RedesignedWorkspace.tsx are modified.
  4. No new test files needed — existing tests cover the routing.

  ### After completing
    git add packages/web/src/workspace/InspectorContent.tsx packages/web/src/workspace/RedesignedWorkspace.tsx
    git commit -m "feat(inspector): FL-05 — family type picker for door + window"
    git push -u origin fl-05-inspector-type-picker

  Do NOT merge to main. The orchestrator will merge after review.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
